uniqueStrengths: Sun auto-weather via Orichalcum Pulse
Dual physical + special pressure
Speed control via Tailwind
Flutter Mane third nuke under Protosynthesis
notes: Dominant Reg I pair. Sun boosts Koraidon's Flare Blitz and Collision Course while Calyrex-Shadow's Astral Barrage spreads special damage. Shared Dark/Fairy weakness managed by partner selection.

uniqueStrengths: No shared weaknesses — complementary defensive profiles
Dual special nukes (both 150+ SpAtk)
Rain removes Calyrex-Shadow's Ground weakness
Cannot be walled by a single Pokémon
notes: No shared weakness is rare for S-tier restricted pairs. Kyogre often runs Choice Scarf (234 Spd) to outspeed Calyrex-Shadow. Main downside: both are special attackers, making physical walls harder to break without support.

uniqueStrengths: Steel/Fairy answers Dark-types walling Astral Barrage
Both max speed — no EV conflict
Fairy STAB neutralizes Dragon resists
Intrepid Sword +1 Atk on entry
notes: Zacian's Steel/Fairy typing directly counters the Dark-types (Incineroar, Grimmsnarl) that immunise Ghost moves. Combined speed covers almost every restricted without Tailwind. Shared vulnerability: Fire and Ground.

uniqueStrengths: Dual-mode team: fast mode (Calyrex-Shadow) or Trick Room mode (Calyrex-Ice)
Opponent cannot prepare for both speed modes simultaneously
165 Atk + 165 SpAtk combined offensive ceiling
notes: Twin Crown pair. Requires dedicated TR setters. Shared Dark/Ghost weakness is a liability. High upside from mode-switching unpredictability at tournaments.

uniqueStrengths: Dual weather forcing — Sun vs Rain pivot strategy
No shared weaknesses
Opponent must commit to one weather answer
notes: Unique anti-meta approach. Lead Kyogre for Rain OHKO opportunities, pivot to Koraidon for Sun offense. Incoherent internal synergy is the trade-off for this unpredictability.

uniqueStrengths: Both benefit from Psychic Terrain (blocks priority)
Hadron Engine + Expanding Force dual spread option
Extremely high special damage ceiling
notes: Electric Terrain and Psychic Terrain are mutually exclusive. Teams typically commit to one. High ceiling but complex execution. Both are physically frail.

uniqueStrengths: Double Sun guarantee — Drought and Orichalcum Pulse overlap
Groudon's Ground typing covers Koraidon's Electric weakness
Precipice Blades spread + Collision Course physical pressure
notes: Double Sun archetype. Groudon provides Drought fallback if Koraidon is KO'd. Both are slow for a non-TR pair — heavily Tailwind dependent.

uniqueStrengths: Rain + Trick Room — two separate game plans
Glacial Lance spread under TR
Blizzard 100% accuracy in Rain
notes: Kyogre does not set TR, so Calyrex-Ice needs dedicated setter support not covered by the restricted pair itself. Grass weakness shared.

uniqueStrengths: Intrepid Sword + Dauntless Shield entry bonuses
Fairy/Fighting + Fighting/Steel dual STAB coverage
Both 148 base Speed — unified speed tier
notes: Sibling pair. Shared Fire/Ground weakness is severe. Zamazenta's offensive ceiling is lower than other restricted options, reducing overall pair pressure.

uniqueStrengths: Dual Psychic/Ghost coverage pressure
Mewtwo Psystrike calculates against physical Defense — bypasses SpDef walls like Cresselia
Calyrex-Shadow As One removes Berries, enabling Mewtwo to bypass Sitrus recovery
Combined 154 + 165 SpAtk — hardest special duo to wall in the format
notes: Shared Ghost and Dark weakness is the main liability — both fold to Kingambit Sucker Punch. Psychic Terrain support boosts both STAB moves and blocks priority against them simultaneously. Farigiraf provides Armor Tail + Psychic Terrain in one slot.

uniqueStrengths: Sun boosts Flare Blitz for Koraidon and Fire-type coverage for Calyrex-Ice's weaknesses
Calyrex-Ice handles Rock and Steel types that threaten Koraidon
Dual physical spread pressure — Glacial Lance + Collision Course both spread damage
Trick Room access gives speed-control flexibility against faster teams
notes: Physical spread pair that operates in both normal and Trick Room modes. Calyrex-Ice anchors slow mode; Koraidon operates in fast mode. Main weakness: Glacial Lance is Ice-type — shared Ice vulnerability means both fold to opposing Ice coverage.

uniqueStrengths: Kyogre Rain negates Zacian Fire weakness — allows Zacian to Tera freely without Fire coverage concern
Zacian Close Combat threatens Steel-types that resist Kyogre Origin Pulse
Zacian at 200 Speed + Kyogre Scarf (181) provides speed control across two restricted slots
Fairy + Water dual STAB — difficult to resist simultaneously
notes: Emerging pair in Reg I. Rain removes Zacian's Fire weakness, enabling more aggressive play. Shared Ground weakness means both fall to Precipice Blades / Earthquake — Great Tusk or Landorus-Therian support helps cover this.

uniqueStrengths: Electric Terrain boosts Miraidon and powers Iron Hands Quark Drive
Calyrex-Ice Trick Room operates independently of terrain — flip mode available
Calyrex-Ice Glacial Lance covers Miraidon's Dragon/Ice weakness targets
Miraidon Thunderbolt handles Water-types that threaten Calyrex-Ice
notes: Four shared weaknesses makes this pair vulnerable to opposing coverage. The appeal is dual mode operation: Electric Terrain fast mode + Trick Room slow mode. Uncommon in top-cut but notable for forcing opponent preparation for two distinct team modes.

uniqueStrengths: Rayquaza Air Lock cancels opposing weather — neutralises Kyogre and Koraidon simultaneously
Calyrex-Shadow As One suppresses Berries on both sides — no Sitrus recovery for opponents under Air Lock
Rayquaza Dragon Ascent removes its Levitate immunity but gains -1 Def: Calyrex covers physical threats
Air Lock + Astral Barrage is uniquely difficult to prepare for in weather-dominated meta
notes: Anti-weather control pair. Rock ×4 weakness on Rayquaza is the biggest liability — Rock Slide flinch can shut down Rayquaza before Air Lock activates. Best used as a weather counter in best-of-3 series where opponent has committed to weather.
